explanation_en: 'bin' is the first person singular (ich) present tense of sein. sein has completely irregular conjugation — 'bin', 'bist', 'ist', 'sind', 'seid', 'sind'.
'sein' means 'to be' in German. It is the most fundamental verb in the language and is also used as an auxiliary to form the Perfekt tense for many verbs.
'sind' is the first person plural (wir) form of sein. Note: 'sind' is also the 3rd person plural — context tells you which one it is.
'ist' is the third person singular (er/sie/es) form of sein. 'Das' acts as a subject here, so we use the 3rd person singular form.
'habe' is the first person singular (ich) present tense of haben. Regular weak verb in the present — just add the standard endings to the stem 'hab-'.
'haben' means 'to have' in German. It is also the most common auxiliary verb used to form the Perfekt (past) tense.
'hat' is the third person singular (er/sie/es) form of haben. Note the irregular shortening: 'habe' → 'hat' (not 'habt').
'haben' is the first person plural (wir) form. The wir-form of haben is identical to the infinitive — 'wir haben'.
'wird' is the third person singular (er/sie/es) form of werden. Note the stem vowel change: werde → wird.
'werden' means 'to become' as a main verb. It is also used as an auxiliary to form the future tense (werden + infinitive) and the passive voice.
'wirst' is the second person singular (du) form of werden. Both du and er/sie/es show stem vowel change: e → i.
'wird' is used here with 'es' (3rd person singular) to describe a change in state. 'Es wird kalt' literally means 'it is becoming cold'.
'kann' is the first person singular (ich) form of können. Modal verbs have no ending in the 1st and 3rd person singular — 'ich kann', not 'ich kanne'.

question.prompt_en: What does this modal verb mean?

explanation_en: 'können' expresses ability or possibility — 'I can', 'I am able to'. It is one of the six German modal verbs and is always followed by an infinitive.
'könnt' is the second person plural (ihr) form of können. Note: ihr form of modals adds -t to the stem (könn- + t).
'Kannst' is the second person singular (du) form of können. In a yes/no question, the verb moves to the first position in the sentence.
'muss' is the first person singular (ich) form of müssen. Like all modal verbs, it has no ending in the 1st person singular — 'ich muss', not 'ich musse'.

question.prompt_en: What does this modal verb mean?

explanation_en: 'müssen' expresses necessity or obligation — something that must be done. Important: 'nicht müssen' means 'don't have to', not 'must not' (that is 'nicht dürfen').
'müssen' is the first person plural (wir) form. Like many modal verbs, the wir-form is identical to the infinitive.
'musst' is the second person singular (du) form of müssen. The du-form adds -t to the stem: muss + t = musst.
'sagst' is the second person singular (du) form of sagen. It is a regular weak verb: stem 'sag-' + ending '-st'.
'sagen' means 'to say'. It is one of the most common communication verbs in German and is used to report speech or state facts.
'sagt' is the third person singular (er/sie/es) form of sagen. Regular weak verbs add -t in the 3rd person singular.
'sagt' is correct here for 'er'. Note that German uses 'sagen' where English might use 'tell' in some contexts, like 'die Wahrheit sagen' (to tell the truth).
'mache' is the first person singular (ich) form of machen. It is a regular weak verb: stem 'mach-' + ending '-e'.
'machen' means both 'to make' and 'to do' in German. It is one of the most versatile and frequently used verbs, appearing in many fixed expressions.
'macht' is the second person plural (ihr) form of machen. Regular weak verbs add -t in the ihr-form.

question.en: What are you doing at the weekend?

explanation_en: 'machst' is the second person singular (du) form. Note: in German, 'machen' covers many situations where English uses 'do', including 'Was machst du?' (What are you doing?).

question.en: I am going to the city today.

explanation_en: 'gehe' is the first person singular (ich) present tense of gehen. It is a strong verb but the present tense is fairly regular.
'gehen' means 'to go' in German. It is a strong verb that uses 'sein' as its Perfekt auxiliary: 'Ich bin gegangen'.
'gehen' is the third person plural (sie) form — identical to the infinitive and wir-form. This is a pattern that holds for most German verbs.
'Gehst' is the du-form of gehen. 'Mitgehen' (to go along) is a separable verb — in a yes/no question, 'mit' moves to the end: 'Gehst du mit?'
'kommst' is the second person singular (du) form of kommen. Strong verb but with a regular present tense: stem 'komm-' + ending '-st'.
'kommen' means 'to come'. Like 'gehen', it uses 'sein' as its Perfekt auxiliary: 'Er ist gekommen'.
'kommt' is the second person plural (ihr) form of kommen. The ihr-form adds -t to the verb stem.
'kommt' is the 3rd person singular (sie = she) form of kommen. Origin is expressed with 'aus + dative': 'aus Berlin', 'aus Deutschland'.

question.en: I want to eat an ice cream.

explanation_en: 'will' is the first person singular (ich) form of wollen. Modal verbs have no ending in the 1st person singular — 'ich will', not 'ich wille'.

question.prompt_en: What does this modal verb mean?

explanation_en: 'wollen' expresses desire or intention — what someone wants to do. It is followed by an infinitive at the end of the clause.
'will' is also the third person singular (er/sie/es) form. In modal verbs, 1st and 3rd person singular are always identical.

question.en: What do you want to become?

explanation_en: 'willst' is the du-form of wollen. The infinitive 'werden' is sent to the end of the clause — this is standard word order with modal verbs in German.
'sehe' is the first person singular (ich) form of sehen. Note that the stem vowel change (e → ie) only occurs in the du and er/sie/es forms.
'sehen' means 'to see'. It is a strong verb with a stem vowel change in the 2nd and 3rd person singular present: 'du siehst', 'er sieht'.
'siehst' is the du-form of sehen. Strong verb with vowel change e → ie in the 2nd and 3rd person singular: 'du siehst', 'er sieht'.
'Siehst' is the du-form with the vowel change e → ie. In a yes/no question the conjugated verb comes first.
'Lass' is the imperative (command) form of lassen for du. The imperative drops the -e ending: 'Lass mich!' (Let me!).
'lassen' means 'to let' or 'to leave (something somewhere)'. It is also used causatively: 'Ich lasse den Arzt kommen' (I have the doctor come).
'lässt' is the 3rd person singular form of lassen. Stem vowel change: a → ä in 2nd and 3rd person singular: 'du lässt', 'er lässt'.

question.de: Sie _____ das Buch auf dem Tisch.
question.en: She leaves the book on the table.

explanation_en: 'lässt' is the 3rd person singular (sie = she). Here 'lassen' means to leave something somewhere. The location uses 'auf + dative': 'auf dem Tisch'.

question.en: He is standing in front of the house.

explanation_en: 'steht' is the third person singular (er/sie/es) form of stehen. Regular present tense: stem 'steh-' + ending '-t'.
'stehen' means 'to stand' — an upright, static position. Compare with 'liegen' (to lie/be lying) and 'sitzen' (to sit).
'stehen' is the wir-form — identical to the infinitive. The wir-form and sie/Sie-form are always the same as the infinitive for regular verbs.

question.en: We are standing in the queue.

explanation_en: 'stehen' is the wir-form. 'In der Schlange stehen' is a fixed expression meaning 'to stand in a queue/line'. 'der Schlange' is dative case after 'in' (static location).
'finde' is the first person singular (ich) form of finden. The -e stem ending means an extra -e is added before personal endings: 'du findest', 'er findet'.
'finden' means 'to find'. It is also commonly used to express opinions: 'Ich finde das gut' (I think that is good / I find that good).
'findest' is the du-form of finden. When a verb stem ends in -d or -t, an extra -e- is inserted before the personal ending: 'find-e-st'.

question.en: Do you also find that interesting?

explanation_en: 'Findest' is the du-form. 'Etwas interessant finden' is a common pattern meaning 'to find something interesting / to think something is interesting'.
'bleibe' is the first person singular (ich) form of bleiben. Strong verb with a regular present tense.
'bleiben' means 'to stay' or 'to remain'. It uses 'sein' as its Perfekt auxiliary: 'Er ist geblieben'.
'bleiben' is the third person plural (sie) form — identical to the infinitive and wir-form. This pattern holds for nearly all German verbs.
'Bleib' is the imperative (command) of bleiben for du. The du-imperative of strong verbs drops the -e: 'Bleib!' (Stay!). 'Bleib ruhig' means 'stay calm'.

question.en: The book is lying on the table.

explanation_en: 'liegt' is the third person singular (es) form of liegen. Location with liegen uses dative prepositions: 'auf dem Tisch' (on the table).
'liegen' describes a flat/horizontal position or geographic location. Contrast with 'legen' (to lay down — the active placing of something).
'liegst' is the du-form of liegen. Regular present tense: stem 'lieg-' + ending '-st'.
'liegt' is used here geographically to mean 'is located in'. 'In Bayern' uses the dative case (static location): 'in + dative' = where something is.
'heiße' is the first person singular (ich) form of heißen. 'Ich heiße Maria' literally means 'I am called Maria' — it's how you introduce your name in German.
'heißen' means 'to be called' (for names) and also 'to mean' (for words/phrases). 'Was heißt das?' = 'What does that mean?'
'heißt' is the 3rd person singular form of heißen. Note: 'du heißt' and 'er heißt' both end in -t — the ß already closes the stem, so no extra -s in 'du heißt'.

question.en: What does that mean in English?

explanation_en: 'heißt' is used here to ask the meaning of a word or phrase. 'Was heißt X?' is an extremely common question when learning German.
'denke' is the first person singular (ich) form of denken. The present tense is completely regular — the irregular change only affects the past participle (gedacht).
'denken' means 'to think'. It is a mixed verb: regular present tense but irregular past participle 'gedacht' (not 'gedenkt').
'denkt' is the ihr-form of denken. Regular present tense: stem 'denk-' + ending '-t'.
'Denkst' is the du-form. 'Denken an + accusative' = to think of/about someone: 'Ich denke an dich' (I think of you). 'mich' is accusative of 'ich'.
'nehme' is the first person singular (ich) form of nehmen. Note: the vowel change e → i only occurs in the 2nd and 3rd person singular — NOT in the ich-form.
'nehmen' means 'to take'. It is a strong verb with a stem vowel change: 'du nimmst', 'er nimmt' (e → i in 2nd and 3rd person singular).
'nimmst' is the du-form of nehmen. Strong verb with vowel change e → i in 2nd person singular: 'nimmst' (not 'nehmst').

question.en: Do you take sugar in your coffee?

explanation_en: 'Nimmst' is the du-form with the vowel change. 'Im Kaffee' = 'in + dem' (contracted) = in the coffee, dative case (static location inside something).

question.en: I think it will rain today.

explanation_en: 'glaube' is the first person singular (ich) form of glauben. It is a regular weak verb. Here it introduces a subordinate clause with 'dass' (that) implied.
'glauben' means 'to believe' or informally 'to think/reckon'. It can take a person in the dative: 'Ich glaube dir' (I believe you).
'glauben' is the 3rd person plural (sie) form — identical to the infinitive. Regular weak verb: stem 'glaub-' + ending '-en'.
'Glaubst' is the du-form. 'Jemandem glauben' takes the dative case: 'Ich glaube dir' (I believe you). 'mir' is the dative form of 'ich'.
'Halt' is the du-imperative of halten. For strong verbs with a/ä vowel change, the imperative uses the regular (non-changed) stem: 'Halt!' not 'Häl!'.
'halten' means 'to hold' (transitive) or 'to stop' (intransitive, e.g. vehicles). It has a vowel change a → ä in the 2nd and 3rd person singular: 'du hältst', 'er hält'.
'hält' is the 3rd person singular of halten. Strong verb with vowel change a → ä in the 2nd and 3rd person singular.
'hält' is the 3rd person singular (der Zug = the train). Used intransitively here to mean 'stops'. 'In Frankfurt' — in + dative for location.

question.en: I am giving you the book.

explanation_en: 'gebe' is the first person singular (ich) form of geben. Note: the vowel change e → i only occurs in the 2nd and 3rd person singular — NOT the ich-form.
'geben' means 'to give'. It takes two objects: the receiver in the dative case and the thing given in the accusative: 'Ich gebe dir das Buch'.
'gibst' is the du-form of geben. Strong verb with vowel change e → i in the 2nd person singular: 'gibst' (not 'gebst').
'gibt' is the 3rd person singular of geben (e → i change). 'ihr' is the dative (to her), 'einen Kuss' is the accusative (the thing given). This is the classic dual-case pattern of geben.

question.en: I am bringing you a coffee.

explanation_en: 'bringe' is the first person singular (ich) form of bringen. The present tense is regular despite bringen being a mixed verb (the irregularity is in the past: brachte, gebracht).
'bringen' means 'to bring'. It is a mixed verb: regular present tense but irregular past: 'brachte' and past participle 'gebracht'.
'bringt' is the 3rd person singular of bringen. Regular present tense: stem 'bring-' + ending '-t'.
'bringen' is the infinitive here, sent to the end of the clause by the modal verb 'kannst'. When a modal verb is used, the main verb goes to the end in the infinitive form.
'lebe' is the first person singular (ich) form of leben. Regular weak verb: stem 'leb-' + ending '-e'.
'leben' means 'to live' in the broad sense (to be alive, to reside somewhere). Compare with 'wohnen' (to live at a specific address).
'lebt' is the ihr-form of leben. Regular weak verb: stem 'leb-' + ending '-t'.
'lebt' is the 3rd person singular (er) form of leben. 'Allein' means 'alone'. Note the nuance: 'Er lebt allein' (he lives alone as a lifestyle) vs 'Er wohnt allein' (he lives alone at his address).
'spreche' is the first person singular (ich) form of sprechen. The vowel change e → i only occurs in the 2nd and 3rd person singular — not the ich-form.
'sprechen' means 'to speak'. Strong verb with vowel change: 'du sprichst', 'er spricht'. Used for speaking languages: 'Ich spreche Deutsch'.
'sprichst' is the du-form of sprechen. Strong verb with vowel change e → i in the 2nd person singular: 'sprichst' (not 'sprechst').
'Sprichst' is the du-form with the vowel change e → i. Language names in German have no article: 'Spanisch sprechen' (not 'das Spanisch sprechen').
'absagen' means 'to cancel'. Separable: the prefix 'ab' detaches — 'Ich sage ab.'

question.en: I have to cancel the meeting.

explanation_en: 'absagen' (to cancel) is separable. With a modal verb the infinitive stays whole at the end.
'absagen' is separable. In present tense main clauses the prefix 'ab' goes to the end: 'Er sagt die Party ab.'

question.de: Wegen des Regens haben wir das Picknick _____.
question.en: Because of the rain we cancelled the picnic.

explanation_en: Perfekt of separable 'absagen': 'ab-ge-sagt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'ansagen' means 'to announce' or 'to declare'. Separable: 'Ich sage an.'

question.en: The host announces the next song.

explanation_en: 'ansagen' is separable: prefix 'an' moves to the end. Present er/sie/es: 'sagt ... an'.
Separable 'ansagen': prefix 'an' goes to end. Ich-form: 'sage ... an'.
Perfekt of separable 'ansagen': 'an-ge-sagt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'aussagen' means 'to testify' (in court) or 'to state'. Separable: 'Ich sage aus.'
'aussagen' is separable: 'aus' goes to end. Er/sie/es-form: 'sagt ... aus'.

question.en: What does that say about his character?

explanation_en: 'aussagen' can mean 'to imply'. Separable: 'es sagt ... aus'.

question.de: Sie hat klar _____, was sie gesehen hat.
question.en: She clearly stated what she had seen.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'aussagen': 'aus-ge-sagt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'aufmachen' means 'to open' (a door, window, shop, etc.). Separable: 'Ich mache auf.'

question.en: Can you please open the door?

explanation_en: 'aufmachen' (to open) is separable, but after a modal verb the infinitive stays together at the end.

question.en: The shop opens at nine o'clock.

explanation_en: Separable verb 'aufmachen': prefix 'auf' goes to end. Es-form: 'macht ... auf'.
Perfekt of 'aufmachen': 'auf-ge-macht'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'zumachen' means 'to close' (informally). Separable: 'Ich mache zu.'
Imperative of 'zumachen': 'mach ... zu!' Prefix 'zu' goes to the end.
Separable 'zumachen': prefix 'zu' moves to end. Er-form: 'macht ... zu'.
Perfekt of 'zumachen': 'zu-ge-macht'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'ausmachen' has two main meanings: to turn off (lights, TV) and to matter/bother. Separable.

question.en: Can you turn off the light?

explanation_en: 'ausmachen' meaning 'to turn off': after a modal verb, the separable infinitive stays whole at the end.
'ausmachen' meaning 'to matter/bother': 'Das macht mir nichts aus'. Prefix 'aus' goes to end.
'ausmachen' also means 'to arrange/agree on'. Perfekt: 'aus-ge-macht'.
'ausgehen' primarily means 'to go out' (socially). Can also mean 'to run out'. Separable, uses 'sein'.
Separable verb: 'wir gehen ... aus'. The prefix 'aus' moves to the end of the main clause.
'ausgehen' also means 'to go out' (of a light/fire). Es-form: 'geht ... aus'.

question.en: We went out to the theatre yesterday.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'ausgehen': 'aus-ge-gangen'. Auxiliary is 'sein' (movement verb).
'losgehen' means 'to set off', 'to start walking', or 'to begin'. Separable, uses 'sein'.
Separable: 'gehen ... los'. Prefix 'los' goes to the end of the clause.
'losgehen' can mean 'to start/begin' for events. Sie-form: 'geht ... los'.

question.en: We set off early in the morning.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'losgehen': 'los-ge-gangen'. Auxiliary is 'sein'.
'eingehen' has several meanings: to enter into (a deal), to shrink (clothing), or to die (of a plant). Context determines meaning.

question.de: Mein Pullover ist in der Wäsche _____.
question.en: My sweater shrank in the wash.

explanation_en: 'eingehen' meaning 'to shrink'. Perfekt: 'ein-ge-gangen'. Auxiliary is 'sein'.

question.en: We do not agree to his demands.

explanation_en: 'eingehen auf' means 'to agree to / to address'. Separable: 'gehen ... ein'.
'eingehen' can mean 'to fold/go under' (of a business). Perfekt with 'sein': 'ist eingegangen'.
'vergehen' means 'to pass' (of time) or 'to fade/diminish'. Inseparable — 'ver' never detaches.
'vergehen' is inseparable. Er/sie/es present: 'vergeht'. Do not split the prefix.
Inseparable prefix 'ver': always written as one word. 'vergeht' = passes/is lost.

question.de: Die Schmerzen sind nach einer Woche _____.
question.en: The pain passed after a week.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'vergehen': 'vergangen' (no 'ge' added). Auxiliary: 'sein'.
'ankommen' means 'to arrive'. Separable and uses 'sein' as auxiliary.
Separable 'ankommen': prefix 'an' moves to end. Er/sie/es-form: 'kommt ... an'.
'ankommen auf' means 'to depend on'. Separable: 'kommt ... an'.

question.de: Wir sind gestern Abend in Berlin _____.
question.en: We arrived in Berlin yesterday evening.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'ankommen': 'an-ge-kommen'. Auxiliary is 'sein'.
'bekommen' means 'to get/receive'. Inseparable — 'be' never detaches. Do not confuse with 'werden' (to become).

question.en: I am getting a package tomorrow.

explanation_en: 'bekommen' is inseparable. Ich-form: 'bekomme'. No prefix separation ever occurs.
'jemandem bekommen' means 'to agree with/suit someone'. Inseparable: always 'bekommt'.
Perfekt of inseparable 'bekommen': just 'bekommen' (no 'ge' added). Auxiliary is 'haben'.
'vorkommen' means 'to occur' (to happen) or 'to seem'. Separable.

question.en: That seems very strange to me.

explanation_en: 'vorkommen' meaning 'to seem': 'Das kommt mir seltsam vor.' Separable: 'kommt ... vor'.
Separable 'vorkommen' = to occur. Sie-form: 'kommen ... vor'.

question.de: So etwas ist mir noch nie _____.
question.en: Something like that has never happened to me before.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'vorkommen': 'vor-ge-kommen'. Auxiliary is 'sein'.
'aussehen' means 'to look' or 'to appear' (in the sense of appearance). Separable.
Separable 'aussehen': prefix 'aus' goes to end. Du-form of sehen is 'siehst': 'siehst ... aus'.

question.en: What will the weather look like tomorrow?

explanation_en: 'aussehen' with 'wie' = to look like. Es-form: 'sieht ... aus'.
Perfekt of 'aussehen': 'aus-ge-sehen'. Auxiliary is 'haben'.
'ansehen' means 'to look at' (someone/something) or 'to watch' (a film). Separable.
Reflexive use: 'sich etwas ansehen' = to watch something. Separable: 'sehen ... an'.

question.en: You can see the tiredness in her face.

explanation_en: 'jemandem etwas ansehen' = to see something in someone's face/manner. 'man sieht ... an'.

question.de: Hast du dir das Museum schon _____?
question.en: Have you already visited the museum?

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'ansehen': 'an-ge-sehen'. Auxiliary: 'haben'. 'sich etwas ansehen' = to visit/view something.
'vorsehen' means 'to plan/schedule' or 'to provide for'. Reflexive: 'sich vorsehen' = to be careful.

question.en: The law provides for a penalty.

explanation_en: Separable 'vorsehen': prefix 'vor' goes to end. Es-form: 'sieht ... vor'.

question.en: The plan provides for three phases.

explanation_en: Separable 'vorsehen': prefix 'vor' to end. Er-form: 'sieht ... vor'.

question.de: Für diesen Fall war keine Lösung _____.
question.en: No solution was planned for this case.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'vorsehen': 'vor-ge-sehen'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'verlassen' means 'to leave' or 'to abandon'. Inseparable. Reflexive: 'sich verlassen auf' = to rely on.

question.en: He leaves the house early in the morning.

explanation_en: 'verlassen' is inseparable. Er-form: 'verlässt'. Never split the prefix.
'sich verlassen auf' = to rely on. Inseparable, reflexive. Ich-form: 'verlasse mich'.

question.de: Sie hat die Stadt vor einem Jahr _____.
question.en: She left the city a year ago.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'verlassen': just 'verlassen' (no 'ge' added). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'auslassen' means 'to omit', 'to skip', or 'to vent (anger)'. Separable.
Imperative of separable 'auslassen': 'Lass ... aus!' The prefix 'aus' goes to the end.

question.en: He vents his anger on me.

explanation_en: 'seinen Ärger an jemandem auslassen' = to vent anger on someone. Separable: 'lässt ... aus'.

question.en: You left out an important sentence.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'auslassen': 'aus-ge-lassen'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'einlassen' means 'to let in/admit'. Reflexive 'sich einlassen auf' = to get involved in. Separable.

question.en: The bouncer does not let us in.

explanation_en: Separable 'einlassen': prefix 'ein' moves to end. Er-form: 'lässt ... ein'.

question.de: Ich _____ mich nicht auf diesen Deal _____.
question.en: I am not getting involved in this deal.

explanation_en: 'sich einlassen auf' = to get involved in. Ich-form: 'lasse mich ... ein'.

question.de: Er hat sich auf ein gefährliches Spiel _____.
question.en: He got involved in a dangerous game.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'einlassen': 'ein-ge-lassen'. Reflexive perfect: 'hat sich ... eingelassen'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'verstehen' means 'to understand'. Inseparable — 'ver' never detaches.
Inseparable 'verstehen'. Ich-form: 'verstehe'. No prefix splitting.

question.en: He gets along well with her.

explanation_en: 'sich verstehen' = to get along. Inseparable. Er-form: 'versteht sich'.
Perfekt of inseparable 'verstehen': 'verstanden' (no 'ge' prefix). Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to get up / to stand up
explanation_en: 'aufstehen' means 'to get up' (from bed/chair) or 'to stand up'. Separable, uses 'sein'.

question.de: Ich _____ jeden Morgen um sieben Uhr _____.
question.en: I get up at seven o'clock every morning.

explanation_en: Separable 'aufstehen': prefix 'auf' goes to end. Ich-form: 'stehe ... auf'.

question.en: When do you get up in the morning?

explanation_en: Separable 'aufstehen'. Du-form of stehen is 'stehst': 'stehst ... auf'.
Perfekt of 'aufstehen': 'auf-ge-standen'. Auxiliary is 'sein'.

correct_answer: to pass (an exam) / to consist of
explanation_en: 'bestehen' has two main uses: to pass (a test) and 'bestehen aus' (to consist of). Inseparable.

question.de: Ich hoffe, dass ich die Prüfung _____.
question.en: I hope that I pass the exam.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'bestehen' = to pass (an exam). Ich-form: 'bestehe'.

question.en: The team consists of five members.

explanation_en: 'bestehen aus' = to consist of. Inseparable. Es-form: 'besteht aus'.

question.de: Er hat die Führerscheinprüfung beim zweiten Versuch _____.
question.en: He passed the driving test on the second attempt.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'bestehen': 'bestanden' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'erfinden' means 'to invent'. Inseparable — 'er' never detaches.
Perfekt of inseparable 'erfinden': 'erfunden' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
Inseparable 'erfinden' = to make up/invent stories. Sie (plural)-form: 'erfinden'.

question.de: Sie _____ eine neue Methode, das Problem zu lösen.
question.en: She invents a new method to solve the problem.

explanation_en: Present tense of inseparable 'erfinden'. Er/sie/es-form: 'erfindet'.
'stattfinden' means 'to take place' (for events). Separable: 'statt' is the separable prefix.

question.en: The concert takes place on Friday.

explanation_en: Separable 'stattfinden': prefix 'statt' goes to end. Es-form: 'findet ... statt'.

question.en: Where does the conference take place?

explanation_en: Separable 'stattfinden'. Sie-form: 'findet ... statt'.

question.de: Die Veranstaltung hat gestern in der Stadtmitte _____.
question.en: The event took place in the city centre yesterday.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'stattfinden': 'statt-ge-funden'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'empfinden' means 'to feel' or 'to perceive' (emotionally). Inseparable.

question.en: I perceive that as an insult.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'empfinden'. Ich-form: 'empfinde'.

question.en: He feels great joy in it.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'empfinden'. Er-form: 'empfindet'.
Perfekt of inseparable 'empfinden': 'empfunden' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to fail to appear / to not happen
explanation_en: 'ausbleiben' means something fails to happen or appear. Separable; uses 'sein'.

question.en: Success won't be long in coming.

explanation_en: 'ausbleiben' = to fail to appear. Separable: 'bleibt ... aus'. Er/es-form: 'bleibt'.

question.en: The consequences won't fail to materialise.

explanation_en: Separable 'ausbleiben'. Sie-form: 'bleiben ... aus'.

question.en: The hoped-for help failed to materialise.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'ausbleiben': 'aus-ge-blieben'. Auxiliary: 'sein'.
'verbleiben' is the formal version of 'bleiben' (to remain). Used in formal writing and letters. Inseparable.
Formal letter closing: 'verbleibe ich' = 'I remain'. Inseparable. Ich-form: 'verbleibe'.

question.en: How do we leave things in this matter?

explanation_en: Inseparable 'verbleiben'. Wir-form: 'verbleiben'. Meaning: 'how do we leave things?'

question.de: Wir sind dabei _____, uns morgen zu treffen.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'verbleiben': 'verblieben' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'sein'.
'überbleiben' means 'to be left over / to remain'. Separable; uses 'sein'.

question.en: What is left over after the meal?

explanation_en: Separable 'überbleiben': prefix 'über' goes to end. Es-form: 'bleibt ... über'.

question.en: There is no other choice left.

explanation_en: Separable 'überbleiben'. Es-form: 'bleibt ... über'.

question.en: Only a few remnants were left over.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'überbleiben': 'über-ge-blieben'. Auxiliary: 'sein'.

correct_answer: to be available / to be present
explanation_en: 'vorliegen' means something is available, on file, or has been submitted. Separable.

question.en: The results are not yet available.

explanation_en: Separable 'vorliegen': prefix 'vor' goes to end. Sie-form: 'liegen ... vor'.
'ein Fehler/Irrtum liegt vor' = there is an error. Separable: 'liegt ... vor'.

question.de: Der Antrag hat bei uns schon _____.
question.en: The application was already on file with us.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'vorliegen': 'vor-ge-legen'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to suggest itself / to be obvious
explanation_en: 'naheliegen' means an idea or conclusion is obvious or suggests itself. Separable.
Separable 'naheliegen': prefix 'nahe' goes to end. Er-form: 'liegt ... nahe'.

question.en: It stands to reason that he lied.

explanation_en: 'es liegt nahe, dass ...' = it stands to reason / it is obvious that ... Separable.

question.de: Diese Lösung hat von Anfang an _____.
question.en: This solution suggested itself from the beginning.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'naheliegen': 'nahe-ge-legen'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'nachdenken über' means 'to think carefully about / to reflect on'. Separable.

question.en: I need to think about that.

explanation_en: After a modal verb, separable 'nachdenken' stays together as an infinitive at the end.

question.en: She thinks for a long time about the problem.

explanation_en: Separable 'nachdenken'. Sie-form of denken: 'denkt ... nach'.

question.de: Er hat lange über seine Entscheidung _____.
question.en: He thought for a long time about his decision.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'nachdenken': 'nach-ge-dacht'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to consider / to bear in mind
explanation_en: 'bedenken' means 'to consider' or 'to bear in mind'. Inseparable — 'be' never detaches.
Inseparable 'bedenken'. After a modal, the infinitive stays whole at the end.

question.de: Man muss _____, dass er krank war.
question.en: One must bear in mind that he was ill.

explanation_en: After 'müssen', the infinitive form is used. Inseparable 'bedenken' stays whole.

question.en: Have you really thought that through?

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'bedenken': 'bedacht' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to rethink / to change one's thinking
explanation_en: 'umdenken' means 'to rethink' or to fundamentally change the way one thinks. Separable.
After a modal verb, separable 'umdenken' stays whole at the end of the clause.
Separable 'umdenken'. Sie-form of denken: 'denkt ... um'.

question.de: Wir haben in den letzten Jahren grundlegend _____.
question.en: We have fundamentally rethought in recent years.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'umdenken': 'um-ge-dacht'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'annehmen' has two main meanings: to accept (an offer) and to assume (suppose). Separable.

question.en: I assume that he is right.

explanation_en: 'annehmen, dass' = to assume that. Separable: 'ich nehme an, dass ...' — prefix 'an' goes to end of that clause.
Separable 'annehmen'. Er-form of nehmen is 'nimmt' (vowel change): 'nimmt ... an'.
Perfekt of 'annehmen': 'an-ge-nommen'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to lose weight / to take off
explanation_en: 'abnehmen' means to lose weight, to take something off/down, or to decrease. Separable.

question.en: He wants to lose five kilos.

explanation_en: 'abnehmen' = to lose weight. After a modal verb, the separable infinitive stays whole at the end.
'abnehmen' = to decrease. Separable. Sie-form of nehmen: 'nimmt ... ab' (vowel change).

question.de: Er hat seit letztem Monat fünf Kilo _____.
question.en: He has lost five kilos since last month.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'abnehmen': 'ab-ge-nommen'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'zunehmen' means to increase (in intensity/size) or to gain weight. Opposite of 'abnehmen'. Separable.
Separable 'zunehmen'. Sie-form of nehmen: 'nimmt ... zu' (vowel change).

question.en: She has been gaining weight lately.

explanation_en: Separable 'zunehmen'. Sie-form: 'nimmt ... zu'.

question.de: Die Zahl der Nutzer hat stark _____.
question.en: The number of users has increased significantly.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'zunehmen': 'zu-ge-nommen'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'unternehmen' means 'to undertake' (a project) or colloquially 'to do something'. Inseparable.
Inseparable 'unternehmen'. After a modal verb, the infinitive stays whole at the end.

question.en: The government does nothing about it.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'unternehmen'. Er/sie/es-form has vowel change: 'unternimmt'.

question.de: Wir haben letzte Woche einen Ausflug _____.
question.en: We went on an excursion last week.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'unternehmen': 'unternommen' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'anhalten' means to stop (a vehicle/person) or to urge/encourage someone. Separable.

question.en: The bus stops at the stop.

explanation_en: Separable 'anhalten'. Er/sie/es-form of halten is 'hält' (vowel change): 'hält ... an'.

question.en: We urge him to be punctual.

explanation_en: 'jemanden zu etwas anhalten' = to urge someone to do something. Separable: 'halten ... an'.
Perfekt of 'anhalten': 'an-ge-halten'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to stop / to delay / to stay
explanation_en: 'aufhalten' means to stop/hold up (progress), to delay, or 'sich aufhalten' = to stay/spend time somewhere. Separable.
After a modal verb, separable 'aufhalten' stays whole as infinitive at the end.

explanation_en: 'sich aufhalten' = to stay/be located. Du-form of halten: 'hältst ... auf'.

question.de: Der Stau hat uns eine Stunde lang _____.
question.en: The traffic jam held us up for an hour.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'aufhalten': 'auf-ge-halten'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'behalten' means 'to keep' (possession) or 'to retain' (information). Inseparable.

question.de: Kannst du das bitte für dich _____?
question.en: Can you please keep that to yourself?

explanation_en: 'für sich behalten' = to keep to oneself. Inseparable, after a modal it stays whole.
Inseparable 'behalten'. Er-form has vowel change: 'behält' (like 'hält').
Perfekt of inseparable 'behalten': 'behalten' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'erhalten' means 'to receive' (formal) or 'to maintain/preserve'. Inseparable.
Perfekt of inseparable 'erhalten': 'erhalten' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'. More formal than 'bekommen'.

question.en: The monument must be well maintained.

explanation_en: 'erhalten' = to maintain/preserve (a monument, tradition). In passive: 'muss erhalten werden'.

question.en: We want to preserve our traditions.

explanation_en: 'erhalten' = to preserve/maintain. Inseparable infinitive after a modal verb.
'ausgeben' means to spend money or to hand out/distribute. Separable.
Separable 'ausgeben'. Ich-form: 'gebe ... aus'.

explanation_en: 'sich als etwas ausgeben' = to pose as something. Perfekt: 'aus-ge-geben'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to give up / to submit / to check in
explanation_en: 'aufgeben' means to give up (a goal), to submit (homework), or to check in (luggage). Separable.

question.en: The teacher assigns a lot of homework.

explanation_en: 'aufgeben' = to assign (homework). Separable. Sie-form of geben: 'gibt ... auf'.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'aufgeben': 'auf-ge-geben'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'angeben' means to state/specify (formally) or colloquially to show off/brag. Separable.
Separable 'angeben'. Sie-form of geben: 'geben ... an'. Formal register.

question.de: Er _____ immer mit seinem neuen Auto _____.

explanation_en: Colloquial 'angeben' = to show off/brag. Er-form of geben: 'gibt ... an'.
Perfekt of 'angeben': 'an-ge-geben'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'mitbringen' means 'to bring along' (when coming to a place). Separable.

explanation_en: After a modal verb, separable 'mitbringen' stays whole as an infinitive at the end.
Separable 'mitbringen'. Er-form: 'bringt ... mit'.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'mitbringen': 'mit-ge-bracht'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.

question.de: Du solltest deine Ideen in die Diskussion _____.
question.en: You should contribute your ideas to the discussion.

explanation_en: After a modal verb, separable 'einbringen' stays whole at the end.

question.en: The investment brought in a lot.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'einbringen': 'ein-ge-bracht'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'verbringen' means to spend (time, a holiday, etc.). Inseparable.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'verbringen'. Wir-form: 'verbringen'. Never split the prefix.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'verbringen'. Er-form: 'verbringt'.

question.de: Sie hat einen schönen Abend mit Freunden _____.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'verbringen': 'verbracht' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'überleben' means 'to survive'. Inseparable (used transitively/intransitively).
'überleben' is inseparable. Perfekt: 'überlebt' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'überleben'. Sie (plural)-form: 'überleben'.
Perfekt of inseparable 'überleben': 'überlebt' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'zusammenleben' means 'to live together' (as a couple, family, or society). Separable.

question.en: How do different cultures live together?

explanation_en: Separable 'zusammenleben'. Sie-form: 'leben ... zusammen'.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'zusammenleben': 'zusammen-ge-lebt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'ansprechen' means to address/speak to someone, to appeal to someone, or to bring up a topic. Separable.

question.de: Er hat sie auf der Straße _____.

question.en: We need to discuss the problem.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'besprechen'. After a modal, the infinitive stays whole at the end.

question.de: Der Chef _____ die Ergebnisse mit dem Team.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'besprechen'. Er-form has vowel change: 'bespricht'.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'besprechen': 'besprochen' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.

'arbeiten' means 'to work'. It is a regular weak verb.

explanation_en: Er/sie/es-form of 'arbeiten': 'arbeitet'. Note the extra 'e' before the ending (arbeite-t) because the stem ends in -t.

question.de: Sie hat für die Prüfung den ganzen Abend _____.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'lernen': 'ge-lernt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'spielen': 'ge-spielt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'kaufen' = to buy. 'verkaufen' = to sell.

question.de: Die Verhandlungen haben zu keinem Ergebnis _____.

'laufen' = to run (or walk in southern German usage). Strong verb with vowel change.

'schreiben' = to write. Strong verb.

explanation_en: Sie-form of 'schreiben': 'schreibt'.

question.de: Er hat ihr einen langen Brief _____.

'lesen' = to read. Strong verb with vowel change.

question.de: Sie hat das Buch in einer Nacht _____.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'lesen': 'ge-lesen'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'fahren' = to drive or to travel (by vehicle). Strong verb.

Du-form of 'fahren' has vowel change: 'fährst'.

question.de: Wir sind im Sommer nach Italien _____.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'fahren': 'ge-fahren'. Auxiliary: 'sein' (movement).

'schlafen' = to sleep. Strong verb with vowel change.

Strong verb 'treffen'. Er-form has vowel change: 'trifft'.

'öffnen' = to open (more formal than 'aufmachen').

'schließen' = to close or to lock. Strong verb.

question.de: Er hat den Laden früher als sonst _____.

'suchen' = to look for, to search. 'finden' = to find.

question.en: I am looking for my key.

question.en: I did not hear the noise.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'hören': 'ge-hört'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'brauchen' = to need. Also 'nicht brauchen + zu' = don't need to.
Weak verb 'brauchen'. Ich-form: 'brauche'.

question.en: You don't need to do that.

explanation_en: 'nicht brauchen + zu-infinitive' = don't need to. Du-form: 'brauchst'.

question.de: Er hat mehr Zeit _____, als erwartet.
question.en: He needed more time than expected.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'brauchen': 'ge-braucht'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'beginnen' = to begin/start. More formal than 'anfangen'.

question.en: The film begins at eight o'clock.

explanation_en: Strong verb 'beginnen'. Er/es-form: 'beginnt'.

question.en: We begin with the first chapter.

explanation_en: Wir-form of 'beginnen': 'beginnen'.
Perfekt of 'beginnen': 'be-gonnen'. Note: inseparable 'be' → no 'ge'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'erklären' = to explain. Inseparable.

question.en: Can you explain that to me?

explanation_en: After 'kannst du mir ... ?' the infinitive goes to the end.

question.en: The teacher explains the grammar rule.

explanation_en: Weak verb 'erklären'. Er-form: 'erklärt'.

question.de: Er hat mir den Weg genau _____.
question.en: He explained the way to me precisely.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'erklären': 'erklärt' (inseparable — no 'ge' prefix). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'entscheiden' = to decide. Inseparable.

question.de: Er _____ sich für den roten Wein.
question.en: He decides on the red wine.

explanation_en: Reflexive 'sich entscheiden für'. Er-form: 'entscheidet sich'.
Er/sie/es-form of 'entscheiden': 'entscheidet'.
Perfekt of 'entscheiden': 'entschieden' (inseparable — no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'setzen' = to put/set (in seated position). Reflexive 'sich setzen' = to sit down.

question.en: I sit down by the window.

explanation_en: Reflexive 'sich setzen' = to sit down. Ich-form: 'setze mich'.

question.de: Er _____ das Kind auf den Stuhl.
question.en: He puts the child on the chair.

explanation_en: Weak verb 'setzen'. Er-form: 'setzt'.

question.en: She sat down next to him.

explanation_en: Perfekt of reflexive 'sich setzen': 'ge-setzt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'stellen' = to place something in an upright/standing position.

question.de: Stell die Flasche auf den Tisch!
question.en: Put the bottle on the table!

explanation_en: 'stellen' for upright objects. Imperative du-form: 'Stell!'
'eine Frage stellen' = to ask a question. Er-form: 'stellt'.

question.de: Ich habe mich der Gruppe vor_____.
question.en: I introduced myself to the group.

explanation_en: 'sich vorstellen' = to introduce oneself. Perfekt: 'vorgestellt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'legen' = to lay something flat/horizontal. Pair: 'liegen' (state), 'legen' (action).

question.de: Er _____ das Buch auf den Tisch.
question.en: He lays the book on the table.

explanation_en: Weak verb 'legen'. Er-form: 'legt'.

question.de: Ich _____ mich für eine Stunde hin.
question.en: I lie down for an hour.

explanation_en: Reflexive 'sich hinlegen' = to lie down. Ich-form: 'lege mich hin'. Note: 'liege' would be the state (I am lying).

question.de: Sie hat die Karten auf den Tisch _____.
question.en: She laid the cards on the table.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'legen': 'ge-legt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'ziehen' = to pull (haben) or to move/relocate (sein).
Strong verb 'ziehen'. Er-form: 'zieht'.

question.en: The family is moving to Berlin.

explanation_en: When meaning 'to move/relocate', 'ziehen' still uses the same conjugation. Sie (family) = sie-form: 'zieht'.

question.de: Wir sind letztes Jahr in eine neue Wohnung _____.
question.en: We moved into a new flat last year.

explanation_en: Perfekt of movement 'ziehen': 'ge-zogen'. Auxiliary: 'sein'.
'warten auf' = to wait for. 'erwarten' = to expect.

question.en: I am waiting for the bus.

explanation_en: Weak verb 'warten'. Ich-form: 'warte'.

question.en: How long have you been waiting?

explanation_en: Du-form of 'warten': 'wartest'.

question.de: Er hat eine Stunde auf sie _____.
question.en: He waited an hour for her.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'warten': 'ge-wartet'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'zahlen' = to pay. 'zählen' (with umlaut) = to count.
Weak verb 'zahlen'. Ich-form: 'zahle'.
Er-form of 'zahlen': 'zahlt'.
Perfekt of 'zahlen': 'ge-zahlt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'verlieren' = to lose. Inseparable 'ver'.
Strong verb 'verlieren'. Ich-form: 'verliere'.
Sie-form of 'verlieren': 'verliert'.
Perfekt of inseparable 'verlieren': 'verloren' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'gewinnen' = to win or to gain. Strong verb, inseparable 'ge'.
Strong verb 'gewinnen'. Sie-form: 'gewinnt'.
Wir-form of 'gewinnen': 'gewinnen'.
Perfekt of 'gewinnen': 'ge-wonnen'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'gehören' = to belong to. Governs dative. Related to 'hören' but different meaning.
'gehören' governs dative: 'mir' (not 'mich').
Sie-form of 'gehören': 'gehört'.
Perfekt of 'gehören': 'ge-hört'. Auxiliary: 'haben'. Note: same past participle as 'hören' — context distinguishes them.
'bedeuten' = to mean, to signify.
Weak verb 'bedeuten'. Es-form: 'bedeutet'.

question.en: That means a lot to me.

explanation_en: Es-form of 'bedeuten': 'bedeutet'. 'das bedeutet mir viel' = that means a lot to me.
Perfekt of 'bedeuten': 'be-deutet' (inseparable — no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'passieren' = to happen (uses 'sein') or to pass/cross (uses 'haben').

question.en: What is happening here right now?

explanation_en: Weak verb 'passieren'. Es-form: 'passiert'.

question.en: That will never happen to me again!

explanation_en: Es-form of 'passieren': 'passiert'. 'jemandem passiert etwas' — dative person.
Perfekt of 'passieren' (to happen): 'passiert'. Auxiliary: 'sein'.
'wechseln' = to change, switch, or exchange.
Weak verb 'wechseln'. Er-form: 'wechselt'.

question.en: Can you change a twenty for me?

explanation_en: After 'kannst du ... ?' the infinitive goes to the end. 'Geld wechseln' = to change money.
Perfekt of 'wechseln': 'ge-wechselt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'rechnen' = to calculate; 'rechnen mit' = to count on/expect.
Weak verb 'rechnen'. Ich-form: 'rechne'.

question.en: The child is very good at maths.

explanation_en: Es-form of 'rechnen': 'rechnet'.
Perfekt of 'rechnen': 'ge-rechnet'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'erscheinen' = to appear (physically), to be published, or to seem. Uses 'sein'.

question.en: The book is published in March.

explanation_en: Strong verb 'erscheinen'. Es-form: 'erscheint'.
'erscheinen' = to seem/appear. Er-form: 'erscheint'.

question.en: He did not appear at the hearing.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'erscheinen': 'er-schienen' (inseparable — no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'sein'.
'versuchen' = to try. Often followed by 'zu + infinitive'.

question.en: I am trying to be punctual.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'versuchen'. Ich-form: 'versuche'.

question.en: He tries it one more time.

explanation_en: Er-form of 'versuchen': 'versucht'.
Perfekt of inseparable 'versuchen': 'versucht' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'ändern' = to change (something). Reflexive 'sich ändern' = to change (oneself).
Weak verb 'ändern'. Er-form: 'ändert'.

question.en: Nothing can be changed about that.

explanation_en: In 'lässt sich nichts ändern' — modal+reflexive+infinitive at the end.
Perfekt of 'ändern': 'ge-ändert'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'anbieten' = to offer. Separable: 'biete an'.
Separable 'anbieten'. Er-form: 'bietet ... an'. Note the vowel change: bieten → bietet.
Separable 'anbieten'. Es-form: 'bietet ... an'.
Perfekt of 'anbieten': 'an-ge-boten'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'benutzen' = to use. Inseparable.
After 'darf ich ... ?' the infinitive goes to the end.
Er-form of inseparable 'benutzen': 'benutzt'.
Perfekt of inseparable 'benutzen': 'benutzt' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'interessieren' = to interest. Reflexive: 'sich interessieren für' = to be interested in.
Weak verb 'interessieren'. Es-form: 'interessiert'.
Reflexive 'sich interessieren'. Ich-form: 'interessiere mich'.

question.de: Er hat sich nie für Sport _____.
question.en: He was never interested in sport.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'interessieren': 'interessiert' (no 'ge' — ends in -ieren). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'sich freuen auf' = to look forward to. 'sich freuen über' = to be happy about.

question.en: I'm looking forward to the weekend.

explanation_en: Reflexive 'sich freuen auf'. Ich-form: 'freue mich'.
'Das freut mich' = That pleases me. Es-form: 'freut'.

question.de: Sie hat sich sehr über das Geschenk _____.
question.en: She was very happy about the gift.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'sich freuen': 'ge-freut'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'fühlen' = to feel (physically or emotionally). Reflexive: 'sich fühlen' = to feel (subjectively).
Reflexive 'sich fühlen'. Ich-form: 'fühle mich'.
Er-form of 'fühlen': 'fühlt'.

question.de: Er hat sich im neuen Job sofort wohlge_____.
question.en: He immediately felt at home in the new job.

explanation_en: Perfekt of reflexive 'sich wohlfühlen': 'wohl-ge-fühlt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'vorschlagen' = to suggest or propose. Separable: 'schlägt vor'.

question.de: Ich _____ vor, eine Pause zu machen.

explanation_en: Separable 'vorschlagen'. Ich-form: 'schlage ... vor'.
Separable 'vorschlagen'. Er-form has vowel change: 'schlägt ... vor'.
Perfekt of 'vorschlagen': 'vor-ge-schlagen'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'behaupten' = to claim or assert (often something unproven). Inseparable.

question.en: He claims to know the truth.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'behaupten'. Er-form: 'behauptet'.

question.en: She holds her own in the discussion.

explanation_en: 'sich behaupten' = to hold one's own. Sie-form: 'behauptet sich'.
Perfekt of inseparable 'behaupten': 'behauptet' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'anerkennen' = to officially acknowledge or recognise. Separable.
Separable 'anerkennen'. Er-form: 'erkennt ... an'.
Separable 'anerkennen'. Er-form: 'erkennt ... an'.
Perfekt of 'anerkennen': 'an-er-kannt'. Auxiliary: 'haben' (active) or 'sein' (passive).
'überzeugen' = to convince someone. Inseparable.

question.en: The argument does not convince me.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'überzeugen'. Es-form: 'überzeugt'.

question.en: He convinces her of his idea.

explanation_en: Er-form of inseparable 'überzeugen': 'überzeugt'.

question.de: Er hat uns alle von seinem Plan _____.
question.en: He convinced us all of his plan.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'überzeugen': 'überzeugt' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'ablehnen' = to reject or decline (an offer, application). Separable.
Separable 'ablehnen'. Er-form: 'lehnt ... ab'.
Separable 'ablehnen'. Es-form: 'lehnt ... ab'.

question.de: Er hat das Angebot ohne Begründung _____.
question.en: He rejected the offer without giving a reason.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'ablehnen': 'ab-ge-lehnt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'versprechen' = to promise. Strong verb, inseparable.

question.en: He promises to be on time.

explanation_en: Strong verb 'versprechen'. Er-form has vowel change: 'verspricht'.
She-form has same vowel change: 'verspricht'.

question.de: Er hat mir hoch und heilig _____, es zu tun.
question.en: He solemnly promised me he would do it.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'versprechen': 'versprochen' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'beweisen' = to prove. Strong verb, inseparable.
Inseparable 'beweisen'. Er-form: 'beweist'.
After 'lässt sich ... ' the infinitive goes to the end.
Perfekt of inseparable 'beweisen': 'bewiesen' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'vermeiden' = to avoid. Strong verb, inseparable.
After 'versuchen zu' the infinitive goes to the end.
Inseparable 'vermeiden'. Sie-form: 'vermeidet'.

question.en: That could easily have been avoided.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'vermeiden': 'vermieden' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'vergleichen' = to compare. 'vergleichen mit' = to compare with.
Inseparable 'vergleichen'. Sie-form: 'vergleicht'.
After 'man kann ... nicht' the infinitive goes to the end.
Perfekt of inseparable 'vergleichen': 'verglichen' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'beschreiben' = to describe. Strong verb, inseparable.
Imperative du-form of 'beschreiben': 'Beschreib!'

question.en: He describes the picture very precisely.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'beschreiben'. Er-form: 'beschreibt'.

question.de: Sie hat die Situation sehr gut _____.
question.en: She described the situation very well.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'beschreiben': 'beschrieben' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'umgehen mit' (separable, sein) = to deal with. 'umgehen' (inseparable, haben) = to bypass/avoid.
'umgehen mit' is separable: prefix 'um' goes to end. 'Er geht ... um'.

question.en: How do you deal with criticism?

explanation_en: Separable 'umgehen mit'. Du-form of gehen: 'gehst ... um'.

question.de: Er ist immer sehr respektvoll mit seinen Mitarbeitern _____.
question.en: He always dealt very respectfully with his colleagues.

explanation_en: Perfekt of separable 'umgehen' (to deal with): 'um-ge-gangen'. Auxiliary: 'sein'.
'auswählen' = to select or choose. Separable.
Separable 'auswählen'. Sie-form: 'wählt ... aus'.
Separable 'auswählen'. Ich-form: 'wähle ... aus'.
Perfekt of 'auswählen': 'aus-ge-wählt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'bewirken' = to bring about, to cause (a result). Inseparable.
Inseparable 'bewirken'. Es-form: 'bewirkt'.

question.en: The measure brings about a lot of good.

explanation_en: Sie-form of inseparable 'bewirken': 'bewirkt'.

question.de: Die Kampagne hat eine große Veränderung _____.
question.en: The campaign brought about a big change.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'bewirken': 'bewirkt' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'zusammenfassen' = to summarise. Separable.
After 'kannst du ... ?' the infinitive goes to the end.

question.en: He summarises the most important points.

explanation_en: Separable 'zusammenfassen'. Er-form: 'fasst ... zusammen'.
Perfekt of 'zusammenfassen': 'zusammen-ge-fasst'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to point out / to refer to
explanation_en: 'hinweisen auf' = to point out or draw attention to. Separable.
Separable 'hinweisen'. Er-form of weisen: 'weist ... hin'.

question.en: The doctor refers to the risks.

explanation_en: Separable 'hinweisen'. Er-form: 'weist ... hin'.

question.de: Er hat mehrfach auf das Problem _____.
question.en: He repeatedly pointed out the problem.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'hinweisen': 'hin-ge-wiesen'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to ask / to demand / to invite
explanation_en: 'auffordern' = to ask or invite someone to do something. Separable.
Separable 'auffordern'. Er-form: 'fordert ... auf'.

question.de: Die Polizei _____ ihn auf, das Fahrzeug zu verlassen.
question.en: The police ask him to leave the vehicle.

explanation_en: Separable 'auffordern'. Sie-form: 'fordert ... auf'.

question.de: Er wurde _____, das Büro zu räumen.
question.en: He was asked to vacate the office.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'auffordern': 'auf-ge-fordert'. Auxiliary: 'haben' (active) / 'sein' (passive).
'schlussfolgern' = to draw a conclusion, to infer. Formal/academic usage.

question.de: Daraus _____ wir, dass er schuldig ist.
question.en: From this we conclude that he is guilty.

explanation_en: Wir-form of 'schlussfolgern': 'schlussfolgern'.

question.en: What can be concluded from this?

explanation_en: After 'kann man ... ' the infinitive goes to the end.

question.de: Die Wissenschaftler haben _____, dass die Theorie korrekt ist.
question.en: The scientists concluded that the theory is correct.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'schlussfolgern': 'ge-schlussfolgert'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to affect / to have an effect
explanation_en: 'sich auswirken auf' = to affect, have an effect on. Always reflexive. Separable.
Separable reflexive 'sich auswirken': 'wirkt sich ... aus'.

question.de: Wie _____ sich das auf unsere Pläne _____?
question.en: How does that affect our plans?

explanation_en: Separable reflexive 'sich auswirken'. Es-form: 'wirkt sich ... aus'.

question.de: Die Krise hat sich stark auf die Wirtschaft _____.
question.en: The crisis strongly affected the economy.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'sich auswirken': 'aus-ge-wirkt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'verpflichten' = to oblige or commit someone. Inseparable.
Inseparable 'verpflichten'. Reflexive: 'sich verpflichten'. Er-form: 'verpflichtet sich'.
Es-form of inseparable 'verpflichten': 'verpflichtet'.
Perfekt of inseparable 'verpflichten': 'verpflichtet' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'betonen' = to emphasise or stress. Inseparable.

question.en: He emphasises the importance of safety.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'betonen'. Er-form: 'betont'.

question.en: I would like to emphasise that ...

explanation_en: 'Ich möchte ... betonen' = I would like to emphasise. Infinitive at end.

question.de: Die Ministerin hat die Dringlichkeit mehrfach _____.
question.en: The minister repeatedly emphasised the urgency.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'betonen': 'betont' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'widersprechen' = to contradict. IMPORTANT: governs dative. Inseparable.
'widersprechen' governs dative: 'ihr' (not 'sie').
Strong inseparable 'widersprechen'. Es-form has vowel change: 'widerspricht'.

question.de: Er hat mir in jedem Punkt _____.
question.en: He contradicted me on every point.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'widersprechen': 'widersprochen' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'überwinden' = to overcome (a fear, obstacle). Inseparable.
Inseparable 'überwinden'. Sie-form: 'überwindet'.

question.en: He overcomes his reluctance and apologises.

explanation_en: Reflexive 'sich überwinden' = to overcome one's reluctance. Er-form: 'überwindet sich'.
Perfekt of inseparable 'überwinden': 'überwunden' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'aufrechterhalten' = to maintain or uphold. Complex separable compound.
Separable compound: 'erhalten ... aufrecht'. Sie-form of 'erhalten': 'erhalten'.

question.de: Es ist schwer, das Gleichgewicht aufrecht_____.
question.en: It is difficult to maintain the balance.

explanation_en: In zu-infinitive form: 'aufrechtzuerhalten' (prefix + zu + verb).

question.en: The peace was maintained with difficulty.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'aufrechterhalten': 'aufrechterhalten'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to take into account / to consider
explanation_en: 'berücksichtigen' = to take into account or consider. Inseparable, formal register.

question.en: Please take all factors into account.

explanation_en: Formal imperative: 'berücksichtigen Sie'. Infinitive form.
After 'muss man' the infinitive goes to the end.

question.en: All objections were taken into account.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'berücksichtigen': 'berücksichtigt' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'hervorheben' = to highlight or emphasise. Separable.

question.en: He highlights the most important points.

explanation_en: Separable 'hervorheben'. Er-form of heben: 'hebt ... hervor'.

question.en: I would particularly like to emphasise that ...

explanation_en: 'ich möchte ... hervorheben' — infinitive at end.

question.de: Die Forscherin hat die Bedeutung des Experiments _____.
question.en: The researcher highlighted the importance of the experiment.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'hervorheben': 'hervor-ge-hoben'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'zusammenarbeiten' = to work together. Separable: 'arbeiten ... zusammen'.
Separable 'zusammenarbeiten'. Wir-form: 'arbeiten ... zusammen'.

question.en: The two companies have been collaborating for years.

explanation_en: Separable 'zusammenarbeiten'. Sie-form: 'arbeiten ... zusammen'.
Perfekt of 'zusammenarbeiten': 'zusammen-ge-arbeitet'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'mitarbeiten' = to contribute or participate (in a project/team). Separable.
Separable 'mitarbeiten'. Er-form: 'arbeitet ... mit'.
Separable 'mitarbeiten'. Sie-form: 'arbeiten ... mit'.

question.en: She actively contributed to the project.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'mitarbeiten': 'mit-ge-arbeitet'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'erarbeiten' = to work out or develop (a plan, solution). Inseparable.
Inseparable 'erarbeiten'. Sie-form: 'erarbeitet'.

question.en: The team works out a new plan.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'erarbeiten'. Es-form: 'erarbeitet'.

question.en: They worked out a solution together.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'erarbeiten': 'erarbeitet' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'erkennen' = to recognise (someone/something) or to realise (a fact). Inseparable.
Inseparable 'erkennen'. Ich-form: 'erkenne'.
Inseparable 'erkennen'. Er-form: 'erkennt'.

question.en: She recognised the problem in time.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'erkennen': 'erkannt' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to get to know / to meet
explanation_en: 'kennenlernen' = to get to know or meet someone for the first time. Separable.

question.en: I'd like to get to know her.

explanation_en: After 'möchte ... gerne' the infinitive goes to the end.

question.en: He gets to know her at a party.

explanation_en: Separable 'kennenlernen'. Er-form: 'lernt ... kennen'.

question.de: Wir haben uns an der Uni _____.
question.en: We got to know each other at university.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'kennenlernen': 'kennen-ge-lernt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'erlernen' = to learn or acquire a skill (more formal than 'lernen'). Inseparable.

question.en: He is learning a craft trade.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'erlernen'. Er-form: 'erlernt'.
After 'lässt sich' the infinitive goes to the end.

question.de: Er hat das Handwerk von Grund auf _____.
question.en: He learned the craft from scratch.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'erlernen': 'erlernt' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to finish one's apprenticeship / to stop learning
explanation_en: 'auslernen' = to finish an apprenticeship, or (idiomatically) 'man lernt nie aus' = one never stops learning.
The proverb 'Man lernt nie aus' uses separable 'auslernen'. Prefix 'aus' goes to end.

question.en: He finishes his apprenticeship next month.

explanation_en: Separable 'auslernen'. Er-form: 'lernt ... aus'.

question.de: Sie hat letztes Jahr als Köchin _____.
question.en: She finished her apprenticeship as a cook last year.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'auslernen': 'aus-ge-lernt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'abspielen' = to play a recording, or 'sich abspielen' = to unfold/take place. Separable.
Separable 'abspielen'. Er-form: 'spielt ... ab'.
Reflexive 'sich abspielen' = to take place. Es-form: 'spielt sich ... ab'.

question.en: He played the video three times.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'abspielen': 'ab-ge-spielt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to join in / to play along
explanation_en: 'mitspielen' = to join in a game, or colloquially to go along with something. Separable.
After 'darf ich' the infinitive goes to the end.
Separable 'mitspielen'. Er-form: 'spielt ... mit'.

question.en: The boat docks in the harbour.

explanation_en: 'anlegen' = to dock (a boat). Es-form: 'legt ... an'.
Perfekt of 'anlegen': 'an-ge-legt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to move house / to change clothes
explanation_en: 'umziehen' = to move to a new home, or 'sich umziehen' = to change clothes. Separable.
Separable 'umziehen'. Wir-form: 'ziehen ... um'.
Reflexive 'sich umziehen' = to change clothes. Ich-form: 'ziehe mich ... um'.

question.de: Er ist letztes Jahr nach Berlin _____.
question.en: He moved to Berlin last year.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'umziehen' (move house): 'um-ge-zogen'. Auxiliary: 'sein'.

correct_answer: to move in / to draft (into army)
explanation_en: 'einziehen' = to move into a new home, or to draft into the army. Separable, uses 'sein'.

question.en: They move into their new flat.

explanation_en: Separable 'einziehen'. Sie-form: 'ziehen ... ein'.

question.en: When are you all moving in?

explanation_en: Separable 'einziehen'. Ihr-form: 'zieht ... ein'.
Perfekt of 'einziehen': 'ein-ge-zogen'. Auxiliary: 'sein'.
'vorziehen' = to prefer. Separable.
Separable 'vorziehen'. Ich-form: 'ziehe ... vor'. Structure: [preferred thing] [thing rejected] vorziehen.
Separable 'vorziehen'. Er-form: 'zieht ... vor'.
Perfekt of 'vorziehen': 'vor-ge-zogen'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to wait and see / to await
explanation_en: 'abwarten' = to wait and see, to bide one's time. Separable.
'Warten wir ab!' = Let's wait and see. Imperative with 'wir': prefix 'ab' goes to end.

question.en: He waits to see how things develop.

explanation_en: Separable 'abwarten'. Er-form: 'wartet ... ab'.

question.en: He patiently waited to see how the situation developed.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'abwarten': 'ab-ge-wartet'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'erwarten' = to expect or await. Inseparable.

question.en: I expect him at three o'clock.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'erwarten'. Ich-form: 'erwarte'.

question.en: He expects a lot from her.

explanation_en: Inseparable 'erwarten'. Er-form: 'erwartet'.
Perfekt of inseparable 'erwarten': 'erwartet' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'bezahlen' = to pay for something (accusative). More direct than 'zahlen'. Inseparable.
Inseparable 'bezahlen'. Er-form: 'bezahlt'. Takes accusative directly.
Ich-form of inseparable 'bezahlen': 'bezahle'.

question.en: He paid the rent on time.

explanation_en: Perfekt of inseparable 'bezahlen': 'bezahlt' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'einzahlen' = to deposit money (at a bank). Separable.
Separable 'einzahlen'. Er-form: 'zahlt ... ein'.

question.de: Ich _____ jeden Monat auf mein Konto _____.
question.en: I deposit money into my account every month.

explanation_en: Separable 'einzahlen'. Ich-form: 'zahle ... ein'.

question.de: Er hat hundert Euro auf sein Konto _____.
question.en: He deposited a hundred euros into his account.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'einzahlen': 'ein-ge-zahlt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to pay out / to be worth it
explanation_en: 'auszahlen' = to pay out (salary, prize), or 'sich auszahlen' = to pay off, to be worth it. Separable.
Separable 'auszahlen'. Er-form: 'zahlt ... aus'.
Reflexive 'sich auszahlen' = to pay off, to be worthwhile. Es-form: 'zahlt sich ... aus'.

question.de: Die Firma hat ihm seinen Lohn sofort _____.
question.en: The company paid out his wages immediately.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'auszahlen': 'aus-ge-zahlt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'sich abwechseln' = to take turns, to alternate. Separable.
Reflexive separable 'sich abwechseln'. Sie-form: 'wechseln sich ... ab'.

question.en: We take turns with the work.

explanation_en: Reflexive separable 'sich abwechseln'. Wir-form: 'wechseln uns ... ab'.

question.de: Sie haben sich bei der Pflege _____.
question.en: They took turns with the care.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'sich abwechseln': 'ab-ge-wechselt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'auswechseln' = to replace or substitute (a player, a part). Separable.
Separable 'auswechseln'. Er-form: 'wechselt ... aus'.
Separable 'auswechseln'. Ich-form: 'wechsle ... aus'.

question.en: He replaced the broken light bulb.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'auswechseln': 'aus-ge-wechselt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'berechnen' = to calculate (costs) or to charge someone. Inseparable.
Inseparable 'berechnen'. Er-form: 'berechnet'.

question.en: How much do you charge for that?

explanation_en: Inseparable 'berechnen'. Sie (formal)-form: 'berechnen'. 'Was berechnen Sie?' = What do you charge?
Perfekt of inseparable 'berechnen': 'berechnet' (no 'ge'). Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to settle accounts / to settle a score
explanation_en: 'abrechnen' = to settle accounts (financial) or colloquially to settle a score. Separable.

question.en: They settle the accounts at the end of the day.

explanation_en: Separable 'abrechnen'. Sie-form: 'rechnen ... ab'.

question.en: I settle the score with him.

explanation_en: Separable 'abrechnen'. Ich-form: 'rechne ... ab'.

question.en: All expenses were settled at the end.

explanation_en: Perfekt of 'abrechnen': 'ab-ge-rechnet'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to feel like (texture) / to feel to the touch
explanation_en: 'sich anfühlen' = to feel like (to the touch). Always reflexive. Separable.
Reflexive separable 'sich anfühlen'. Es-form: 'fühlt sich ... an'.
Reflexive 'sich anfühlen'. Es-form: 'fühlt sich ... an'.
Perfekt of 'sich anfühlen': 'an-ge-fühlt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.

correct_answer: to empathise / to feel for someone
explanation_en: 'mitfühlen' = to empathise, to feel for someone. Separable.
Separable 'mitfühlen'. Ich-form: 'fühle ... mit'.
Separable 'mitfühlen'. Er-form: 'fühlt ... mit'.
Perfekt of 'mitfühlen': 'mit-ge-fühlt'. Auxiliary: 'haben'.
'fassen' means to grasp or seize. Also: 'sich fassen' = to compose oneself.
'fassen' in the infinitive after a modal verb. 'Er konnte kaum fassen' = he could barely grasp/believe it.

question.de: Sie _____ sich ein Herz und fragt ihn.
question.en: She plucks up courage and asks him.

explanation_en: 'sich ein Herz fassen' = to pluck up courage. Er-form reflexive: 'sie fasst sich'.
wir-form of 'fassen' is identical to the infinitive: 'wir fassen'.
'heben' means to lift or raise. Strong verb with past participle 'gehoben'.
er-form of 'heben' is 'hebt'. Regular present conjugation despite being a strong verb.
After 'kannst du ...?' the infinitive goes to the end. 'heben' = to lift.
ich-form of 'heben' is 'hebe'. Add -e to the stem 'heb-'.
'bieten' means to offer. Strong verb: PP 'geboten'. Often used with dative (person) + accusative (thing).
er/es-form of 'bieten' is 'bietet'.

question.de: Ich kann dir nur das _____, was ich habe.
question.en: I can only offer you what I have.

explanation_en: After a modal verb the infinitive goes to the end. 'bieten' stays as infinitive after 'kann'.
er-form of 'bieten' is 'bietet'. Add -et to stem 'biet-' (e-insertion before t).
'schlagen' means to hit or beat. Strong verb with umlaut in 2nd/3rd person singular.
er-form of 'schlagen' is 'schlägt' — note the umlaut (a → ä).

question.de: Er _____ den Nagel in die Wand.
question.en: He hammers the nail into the wall.

explanation_en: er-form with umlaut: 'schlägt'. Takes accusative direct object.
du-form of 'schlagen' is 'schlägst' — umlaut applies to both du and er/sie/es forms.
'wählen' means to choose or elect. Used for democratic elections and everyday choices.
wir-form of 'wählen' is 'wählen' — identical to the infinitive.

question.en: She always chooses the wrong option.

explanation_en: sie (sg.)/er-form of 'wählen' is 'wählt'. Regular weak verb.
ich-form of 'wählen' is 'wähle'. Add -e to stem 'wähl-'.
'nutzen' means to use or make use of. Synonym of 'benutzen'. Also: 'Es nutzt nichts' = it's no use.
er-form of 'nutzen' is 'nutzt'. Regular weak verb.

question.en: I want to make better use of my time.

explanation_en: After 'möchte + infinitive' goes to the end. 'nutzen' = to use.
sie (plural) form of 'nutzen' is 'nutzen' — identical to the infinitive.
'fordern' means to demand. Stronger than 'bitten' (to ask politely). 'fordern von' = to demand from someone.
sie-form of 'fordern' is 'fordert'. Regular weak verb.

question.en: The task demands a lot of concentration.

explanation_en: 'fordern' can mean 'to require' when used with tasks or challenges. er-form: 'fordert'.
er-form of 'fordern' is 'fordert'. Note: stem is 'forder-', add -t for er/sie/es.
'scheinen' has two main meanings: 'to shine' (sun) and 'to seem/appear' (with zu + infinitive).

question.en: The sun is shining very brightly today.

explanation_en: es/sie-form of 'scheinen' is 'scheint'. Used for both 'to shine' and 'to seem'.
'scheinen + zu + infinitive' = to seem to be. er-form: 'scheint'.
es-form of 'scheinen' is 'scheint'. Same as er/sie forms.
'deuten' means to interpret or to point. 'deuten auf etw.' = to point at something; 'etw. deuten' = to interpret something.
'deuten auf' = to point at. er-form: 'deutet'. Takes accusative after 'auf'.

question.en: How do you interpret this sign?

explanation_en: du-form of 'deuten' is 'deutest'. Note the -e- inserted before -st: 'deut-e-st'.
wir-form of 'deuten' is 'deuten' — same as the infinitive.

correct_answer: to have an effect / to work
explanation_en: 'wirken' means to have an effect, to work (as in medicine or a plan), or to appear/seem.
es-form of 'wirken' is 'wirkt'. 'Das Medikament wirkt' = the medication has its effect.
'wirken' used as 'to appear/seem'. er-form: 'wirkt'. 'Er wirkt entspannt' = he comes across as relaxed.
es-form of 'wirken' is 'wirkt'. Regular weak verb.
'lehnen' means to lean. Used with 'an + accusative' = to lean against. Reflexive 'sich lehnen' = to lean oneself.
er-form of 'lehnen' is 'lehnt'. Reflexive use: 'sich lehnen gegen'.

question.de: Sie _____ das Fahrrad an den Baum.
question.en: She leans the bike against the tree.

explanation_en: Transitive use: 'etw. an etw. lehnen' (accusative). sie-form: 'lehnt'.
du-form of 'lehnen' is 'lehnst'. Regular weak verb.
'weisen' means to point or direct someone. Strong verb. 'jdm. den Weg weisen' = to show someone the way.
er-form of 'weisen' is 'weist'. 'jdm. den Weg weisen' = to direct someone (dative + accusative).

question.en: She directs the visitor to the exit.

explanation_en: sie-form of 'weisen' is 'weist'. Used for directing people to a place.
er-form of 'weisen' is 'weist'. Strong verb, but present tense is regular.
'gleichen' means to resemble or be equal to. Takes the dative case: 'Er gleicht ihm' (not 'ihn').
er-form of 'gleichen' is 'gleicht'. Requires dative: 'seinem Vater' (dative).

question.en: The two houses barely resemble each other.

explanation_en: sie (plural)-form of 'gleichen' is 'gleichen'. Reflexive use: 'sich gleichen'.
sie (plural)-form of 'gleichen' = 'gleichen'. Same as the infinitive.
'meiden' means to deliberately avoid something or someone. Strong verb, PP: 'gemieden'.
er-form of 'meiden' is 'meidet'. Strong verb with regular present tense conjugation.
After 'zu' the infinitive is used. 'meiden' stays unchanged. Takes accusative direct object.
du-form of 'meiden' is 'meidest'. The -e- is inserted: 'meid-e-st'.
